diagrams.title: RBAC Core Components and Relationships
diagrams.description: Shows the three main components of RBAC - Users, Roles, and Permissions - and how they relate through User-Role Assignment and Role-Permission Assignment
elements: Arrows showing: Users → Roles (URA), Roles → Permissions (RPA)
diagrams.purpose: Understanding the fundamental structure of RBAC and how access flows from users through roles to permissions

diagrams.title: Role Hierarchy in DRBAC
diagrams.description: Illustrates how senior roles inherit permissions from subordinate roles in a hierarchical structure
elements: Manager role in middle (inherits from Employee)
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing how role hierarchy simplifies permission management through inheritance

diagrams.title: DRBAC vs Traditional Access Control
diagrams.description: Comparison showing how DRBAC simplifies access control compared to traditional user-by-user permission assignment
elements: Role groupings showing multiple users per role
Permission groupings showing multiple permissions per role
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrating the administrative efficiency of DRBAC over traditional access control methods

diagrams.title: Access Control Models Spectrum
diagrams.description: Shows where DRBAC sits on the access control spectrum between DAC and RBAC
elements: Arrows showing discretionary elements and role-based elements
Key features listed for each model
diagrams.purpose: Understanding DRBAC as a hybrid model combining the best of DAC and RBAC
